dataset: Bridge Command Human Factors Experiment Batch 2026

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates Base Scenario Config files at this path (lines 11-50)
setup_task.sh references this directory in experimental brief (line 64) and export_result.sh line 6
experimental_design.txt brief in setup_task.sh specifies 'Low=0.5nm' visibility (lines 56-86)
experimental_design.txt specifies 'Total Vessels: 1 (Target Only)' for Cond_A (line 68)
experimental_design.txt specifies 'Low=0.5nm' for Cond_B (line 70-71)
experimental_design.txt specifies 'Total Vessels: 4 (Target + 3 Distractors)' for Cond_B (line 72)
experimental_design.txt specifies 'High=12.0nm' for Cond_C (lines 74-76)
experimental_design.txt specifies 'Total Vessels: 1 (Target Only)' for Cond_C (line 76)
experimental_design.txt specifies 'High=12.0nm' for Cond_D (lines 78-80)
experimental_design.txt specifies 'Total Vessels: 4 (Target + 3 Distractors)' for Cond_D (line 80)
setup_task.sh creates manifest file at this path (line 85) and export_result.sh references it (line 5)

setup_task.sh sets Setting="Solent" in base environment.ini (line 17). Bridge Command documentation does not list 'Solent' as a pre-installed world area. Official documentation (bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/4.7/Scenario.php) only mentions SantaCatalina as example. 'Solent' not verified as available in standard Bridge Command installation.

Base scenario in setup_task.sh has VisibilityRange=12.0 (line 23). VisibilityRange parameter confirmed in Bridge Command documentation (bridgecommand.co.uk) as valid parameter in environment.ini taking nautical mile values.
Base othership.ini in setup_task.sh has Number=1 (line 41). Number parameter confirmed in Bridge Command documentation as valid vessel count parameter.
Bridge Command documentation at bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/4.1/Scenario.php confirms VisibilityRange is 'The maximum distance at which objects are visible through fog/haze, in nautical miles'
experimental_design.txt in setup_task.sh defines Factor A: Visibility (Low=0.5nm, High=12.0nm). Value 0.5nm represents severe fog/restricted visibility, realistic for maritime training scenarios.

experimental_design.txt defines visibility high value as 12.0nm, representing clear conditions. Base scenario also uses 12.0nm initially. Both values are realistic nautical mile measurements used in maritime simulation.
experimental_design.txt defines Factor B: Traffic Density (Low=1 vessel, High=4 vessels). Low traffic is 1 target vessel only.
experimental_design.txt defines high traffic as 4 vessels (Target + 3 Distractors). Task description confirms adding 3 additional distractor vessels for high-traffic conditions.

setup_task.sh defines InitialLat=50.78 for ownship and InitLat(1)=50.78 for othership (lines 31, 43). These coordinates are in the Solent maritime area (UK), verified to be near Port Solent at 50.843°N.
setup_task.sh defines InitialLong=-1.12 for ownship and InitLong(1)=-1.08 for othership (lines 32, 44). Coordinates correspond to Solent area (UK), verified near Port Solent at 1.101°W.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates synthetic human factors experiment scenarios using Bridge Command maritime simulator. The metadata accurately specifies a 2x2 factorial design with Visibility (Low=0.5nm, High=12.0nm) and Traffic Density (Low=1 vessel, High=4 vessels) factors, creating four scenario conditions. All parameters (VisibilityRange, Number of vessels, visibility values, traffic counts) match Bridge Command's documented INI file specifications. Scenarios are derived from a base configuration set in the Solent maritime area (UK coordinates 50.78°N, -1.12°W) with specific visibility and vessel configurations. The manifest and directory structure paths are correctly specified. One unverified claim: 'Solent' as a world setting is not documented in official Bridge Command materials, though it may be available as a custom or addon world area.
